correction_reason: Initial audit missed explicit inline gate at bot_core.py:590-600. This gate is active and enforces LATE_INNING_BLOCK in the live bridge entry path.
verification_performed: Confirmed LATE_INNING_BLOCK exists in .env at line 99: LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7
Traced recommendation creation path in recommendation_api.py to confirm it populates inning field but does not read LATE_INNING_BLOCK config
Traced bridge approval path in model_bridge.py to confirm it preserves inning field but does not read LATE_INNING_BLOCK config
Traced bot_core.py bridge loop (lines 523-701) which calls get_approved_intents() and iterates through returned intents
Located explicit inline gate at bot_core.py:590-600 that reads LATE_INNING_BLOCK and enforces late-inning rejection

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK is loaded from config in bot_core.py at module level (line 107) and is actively used in the live entry path
The active live entry path is recommendation_api.py -> model_bridge.py -> bot_core.py bridge loop, with inline late-inning enforcement before check_entry_gates()
proven.evidence: bot_core.py lines 522-600: get_approved_intents() returns intents, bridge loop processes each, late-inning gate fires at line 590 before entry gating at line 632

proven.point: The explicit inline gate at bot_core.py:590-600 reads LATE_INNING_BLOCK and rejects intents where inning >= LATE_INNING_BLOCK
proven.evidence: Lines 585-600: extracts intent.get('inning'), validates to int, compares against LATE_INNING_BLOCK, logs BRIDGE GATE REJECT [late_inning_block], increments _guard_block_count, and continues

proven.point: Inning data is preserved end-to-end from recommendation creation through bridge intent to the enforcement gate
proven.evidence: recommendation_api.py includes inning in returned Recommendation, model_bridge.py preserves it in intent dict, bot_core.py extracts it at line 585 for gating

proven.point: LATE_INNING_BLOCK enforcement is active in the live entry path and successfully blocks late-inning entries via bridge loop inline gate, not delegated to risk.py
proven.evidence: bot_core.py:590 gate fires inside bridge loop iteration, sets _guard_block_count += 1 and appends to loop_guard_reasons, proving it is reached in live execution flow

ruled_out: Ruled out: LATE_INNING_BLOCK config value missing or not loaded
Ruled out: inning data missing from recommendations
Ruled out: inning data lost between bridge approval and entry gating
Ruled out: LATE_INNING_BLOCK not wired into the live entry path
recommended_next_step: The wiring is confirmed active. Next investigation should verify whether the gate triggers correctly in live conditions with real game state data. If late-inning entries still occur despite the gate, audit should check: (1) whether game state snap.inning is correctly populated from ESPN, (2) whether LATE_INNING_BLOCK value (7) is appropriate for the intended behavior, (3) whether stale inning data causes gate bypass, (4) whether the gate is firing but being overridden by downstream logic.
